prompt:
Create a therapeutic audio session for the following goal. Write the full script in English, approximately 10 minutes long when read aloud.

## Goal
Title: Build confidence and positive self-image
Description: Help Noah recognize his strengths and feel proud of who he is.

## Person
This is for Noah (age 7).
Developmental Tier: MIDDLE_CHILDHOOD

## Research Evidence
The following research papers inform the therapeutic techniques to use:

No research papers available yet. Use general evidence-based therapeutic techniques.

## Instructions
- Create a complete, flowing therapeutic audio script
- Incorporate specific techniques and findings from the research above
- When clinical notes are available, weave their insights and observations into the session
- Personalize for Noah (age 7) (developmental tier: MIDDLE_CHILDHOOD)
- Target duration: 10 minutes when read aloud at a calm pace
- Write in English
- Follow the therapeutic audio content structure (warm introduction, understanding the challenge, guided practices, integration)
- Include a brief mention that a parent, caregiver, or professional can provide additional support if needed
- IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any markdown formatting (no **, ##, *, bullet points, or bold/italic syntax). Write plain spoken prose only, as the script will be read aloud by a text-to-speech engine
script:
Welcome, Noah. I'm so glad you're here with me today. We're going to go on a little adventure, a special one all about you. We're going to discover some of the amazing things that make you, you. Find a cozy spot to sit or lie down, and let's get ready to begin.

You know, sometimes it can be hard to see how awesome we really are. Everyone, even grown-ups, sometimes forgets about their own superpowers. But you have them, Noah. Today, we're going to be like explorers, looking for clues about what makes you strong, kind, and special.

First, let's get our bodies ready for this adventure. Take a nice, slow breath in through your nose... like you're smelling a yummy cookie. And let it out through your mouth... like you're blowing on a dandelion to make a wish. Let's do that two more times. Breathe in the good, calm air... and breathe out any wiggles or worries. Good job.

Now, I want you to imagine something. Picture your very favorite thing to do. Maybe it's building with blocks, drawing a picture, kicking a ball, reading a story, or helping someone. Can you see it in your mind? What does it feel like when you're doing that thing? Do you feel focused? Happy? Strong? Just notice that feeling for a moment. That feeling is a clue. It's a clue about one of your strengths.

Let's play a game called "I Am." I'm going to say some sentences, and you just think in your head if they feel true for you. There's no right or wrong answer. Just see what your heart says. Ready?

I am a good friend.
I am brave when I try new things.
I am creative.
I am a helper.
I am a good listener.
I am strong in my body.
I am a learner.
I am someone who can make others smile.

Did any of those feel extra true? Maybe one or two made you think, "Yeah, I do that!" Those are your superpowers, Noah. Let's pick one. Think of one time recently when you were a good friend, or when you were brave, or when you were creative. Can you remember what you did? Picture it like a movie in your mind. See yourself doing that good thing. How did it feel in your body? Maybe warm, or tingly, or just good. Let's give that feeling a color. What color is your brave feeling or your kind feeling? Imagine that color is like a light, right in the middle of your chest. With every breath, imagine that colored light gets a little brighter, shining out from you.

Now, put your hand on your heart. Feel it beating? That's your heart, always working for you. Let's say something kind to ourselves, just in our minds. Repeat after me in your thoughts: "I am enough." "I have good things inside me." "I am proud of myself for trying."

We're going to finish our adventure by making a secret treasure. In your mind, I want you to build a treasure chest. It can look any way you want—wood, gold, even a cool box with stickers. Now, inside that chest, I want you to put the feelings we found today. Put in that colored light. Put in the memory of when you used your superpower. Put in the kind words you just said to yourself. You can always open this treasure chest in your mind whenever you need to remember how awesome you are. It's your secret power source.

So, Noah, today we discovered that you have strengths, like being a good friend, or brave, or creative. We found a good feeling and gave it a color. And we made a treasure chest to keep all these good things safe inside you. You can visit this treasure anytime. Maybe before bed, or if you're feeling unsure, just take a deep breath and remember your colored light.

You are an amazing person, with so much good inside. Keep discovering your superpowers. And remember, it's always okay to talk to a parent, a caregiver, or another grown-up you trust about your feelings. They can help you see your strengths too.

Great job today, explorer. I'm so proud of you for taking this time. Carry that good, bright feeling with you.
